title: Invalid byte squence
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I freshly installed Fedora 30 and used it to switch from Docker to Podman, but when I run a container, I get following error message:

```
    podman run --rm hello-world:latest 
    option parsing failed: Invalid byte sequence in conversion input
    Error: exit status 1
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I'm not sure if it's reproducable, but the error appears with all containers I tried to run. Nothing in the container logs, container were created, but show no output.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
    option parsing failed: Invalid byte sequence in conversion input
    Error: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
    It should show the container output.
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      commit: e3b4c1108f7d1bf0d09ab612ea09927d9b59b4e3

    Backing Filesystem: extfs

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical on Lenovo notebook.


**Output of locale**

```
LANG=en_US.UTF-8
LC_CTYPE=en_US.UTF-8
LC_NUMERIC="en_US.UTF-8"

**On the same system following happens when I run that with docker:**

```
Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.

To generate this message, Docker took the following steps:
 1. The Docker client contacted the Docker daemon.
 2. The Docker daemon pulled the "hello-world" image from the Docker Hub.
    (amd64)
 3. The Docker daemon created a new container from that image which runs the
    executable that produces the output you are currently reading.
 4. The Docker daemon streamed that output to the Docker client, which sent it
    to your terminal.

To try something more ambitious, you can run an Ubuntu container with:
 $ docker run -it ubuntu bash

Share images, automate workflows, and more with a free Docker ID:
 https://hub.docker.com/

For more examples and ideas, visit:
 https://docs.docker.com/get-started/
```

comments.body: I can't find that error string anywhere in our repo or Cobra's repo - no idea where this is coming out of.
Works fine here on latest master on my system. Identical locale. Works both as root and rootless.
This could be coming from `conmon`, relaying an error it's getting back from `libglib-2.0.so.0`.
Conmon seems like a reasonable guess here
Installing a VM with default an try again. Thanks for the hint. Will come back when the vm is running.

It is not reproducable. I installed FC30 minimal with just podman and it works like a charm. 
So it is related to something I added (zsh, i3wm, tmux or whatever). I will dig into it, thanks for all tips. 

I found the error. I had a copy&paste error in `.zshrc`:

```
export XDG_DATA_HOME=/home/stoeps/.local/share¬   
```
I configured vim to show the line feed char and in that line I had 2 so I copied it somehow. The character was also in `~/.config/containers/libpod.conf` and `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` I removed it and then podman worked normally. Sorry and thanks for your help.
